content.heading: showtell
content.subtitle: A motion engine for agents — how it works

scenes.narration: showtell is a local, repo-aware renderer. Instead of writing you an answer, a coding agent makes you a short narrated video.

content.heading: The idea
points: The agent authors only a spec.json — never pixels or ffmpeg.
It renders locally, so it reads your real git diff and files.
One spec becomes an mp4 in both 16:9 and 9:16.

scenes.narration: The whole contract is a single JSON file. The agent describes scenes and narration; the renderer owns every pixel. Because it runs locally, it can read your actual code.
Here's the load-bearing rule. A code scene carries a file and line range — a reference, never pasted source. The renderer reads the live bytes, so the code on screen is always ground truth.
And because it renders untrusted, agent-authored specs, we just hardened the capture boundary — this is the real diff that closed a path-traversal hole.
Under the hood it's six small packages with a one-way dependency flow: core feeds compose and capture, which feed render, which the CLI drives.
To use it: validate your spec, render it to an mp4, or preview it on a local watch page. That's it — this very video was made by the tool explaining itself.
